new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a beverage indicating that it contains fat derived from dairy products of CN headings 0401 to 0404, specifically fat content of 0.2% or more by weight, as characteristic of 'filled milk' or similar dairy-fat-containing non-alcoholic beverages.

new_classes.label_en: Other non-alcoholic beverages containing fat from dairy products of headings 0401–0404, of a fat content of 0.2% or more by weight (CN 2202 99 95)
new_classes.label_de: Andere nichtalkoholische Getränke mit Fettgehalt aus Erzeugnissen der Positionen 0401 bis 0404, mit einem Fettgehalt von 0,2 GHT oder mehr (KN 2202 99 95)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-alcoholic beverage that is not a water, mineral water or flavoured/sweetened water (CN 2202 10), and that contains fat obtained from dairy products of CN headings 0401 to 0404 at a content of 0.2% or more by weight. Includes commercially known 'filled milk' (a beverage based on skimmed or powdered skimmed milk to which refined vegetable fats are added) provided it is ready for consumption as a beverage.

new_properties.label_en: fat from dairy products content by weight percent

coverage_explanation:
The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the beverage is non-alcoholic, (2) it falls under the 'other non-alcoholic beverages' branch of 2202 99 (not flavoured/sweetened water of 2202 10), and (3) it contains fat derived from dairy products of headings 0401–0404 at ≥ 0.2% by weight. The new 'FilledMilkFatFromDairyQuality' class and the 'fatFromDairyByWeightPercent' data property encode these criteria. 

Coverage gaps reducing score from 1.0:
- The legal text specifies the fat threshold boundary structure as a range (0.2% or more) within a broader set '22029991 to 22029999', implying sibling codes 22029991 (< 0.2% fat) and 22029995 (≥ 0.2% fat) are discriminated solely by fat content threshold. The upper bound of the 22029995 subheading relative to 22029991 is captured by the minInclusive 0.2 restriction, but the sibling code 22029991 (fat content < 0.2%) is not yet modelled in the TBox, so a full disjointness complement axiom against it cannot be made precisely.
- The specific notion of 'filled milk' as a consumption-ready beverage based on skimmed/powdered skimmed milk with added refined vegetable fats is described in the note but cannot be fully formalised without ingredient-composition classes not yet present in the TBox (skimmed milk base, added vegetable fats).
- The 'ready for consumption' criterion is implicit in the Beverage superclass but not explicitly formalised as a quality restriction.
